parentQuestion: Stimulation of β2 receptors in the bronchi causes ___.

options: Adrenergic receptors that bind norepinephrine and epinephrine
scaffolds.explain: β2 receptors are ADRENERGIC receptors that bind norepinephrine and especially epinephrine. They're found in bronchial smooth muscle, blood vessels, and other tissues.

wrongExplains.0: Cholinergic receptors (nicotinic, muscarinic) bind ACh. β2 receptors bind catecholamines.
wrongExplains.2: Dopamine has its own receptor family. β2 are specifically adrenergic.
wrongExplains.3: Serotonin has its own receptor family. β2 are adrenergic receptors.

options: Airways that carry air to and from the lungs
scaffolds.explain: Bronchi and bronchioles are AIRWAYS — tubes that branch from the trachea to carry air into the lungs. Their walls contain smooth muscle that can change their diameter.

wrongExplains.0: Blood vessels carry blood. Bronchi carry air.
wrongExplains.2: Digestive tubes process food. Bronchi are part of the respiratory system.
wrongExplains.3: Nerve pathways conduct electrical signals. Bronchi are tubes for airflow.

scaffolds.q: Bronchioles have smooth muscle in their walls. What does this mean?
scaffolds.explain: Smooth muscle allows bronchioles to change diameter: CONTRACT (bronchoconstriction) to narrow, or RELAX (bronchodilation) to widen. This controls airflow resistance.

wrongExplains.0: Smooth muscle specifically enables diameter changes. That's its function.
wrongExplains.2: Bronchioles are soft tubes with smooth muscle, not rigid bone.
wrongExplains.3: Bronchioles function continuously to adjust airflow based on body needs.

scaffolds.q: What happens when bronchial smooth muscle RELAXES?
scaffolds.explain: Relaxed smooth muscle means the airway WIDENS (bronchodilation). This allows more air to flow in and out, increasing ventilation capacity.

wrongExplains.0: Narrowing occurs with smooth muscle CONTRACTION, not relaxation.
wrongExplains.2: Relaxation opens airways; it doesn't close them.
wrongExplains.3: Relaxation definitely increases diameter — that's the effect of removing muscle tension.

scaffolds.q: During exercise or stress, you need ___.
options: More air flow to deliver more oxygen
scaffolds.explain: Exercise increases oxygen demand. Your body needs MORE AIR FLOW to bring in oxygen and remove CO2. This is achieved by bronchodilation.

wrongExplains.0: Less airflow would create oxygen debt. Exercise requires more airflow.
wrongExplains.2: Closing airways would cause suffocation. Exercise opens airways wider.
wrongExplains.3: Breathing increases during exercise, not decreases.

scaffolds.q: The sympathetic system (fight-or-flight) causes bronchi to ___.
scaffolds.explain: During fight-or-flight, bronchi DILATE to allow maximum airflow. If you're running from danger, you need all the oxygen you can get. Sympathetic = open airways.

wrongExplains.0: Constriction is a parasympathetic effect. Sympathetic causes bronchodilation.
wrongExplains.2: Complete closure would prevent breathing. The body needs MORE air during stress.
wrongExplains.3: There IS a change — airways open wider during sympathetic activation.

scaffolds.q: What receptor mediates bronchodilation when stimulated by epinephrine?
scaffolds.explain: β2 RECEPTORS on bronchial smooth muscle mediate bronchodilation. When epinephrine (or norepinephrine) binds to β2 receptors, the smooth muscle relaxes and airways widen.

wrongExplains.0: α1 receptors cause vasoconstriction in blood vessels, not bronchodilation.
wrongExplains.2: Muscarinic receptors in bronchi cause bronchoconstriction (parasympathetic), not dilation.
wrongExplains.3: Nicotinic receptors are at ganglia, not on bronchial smooth muscle.

scaffolds.q: Asthma rescue inhalers (like albuterol) work by ___.
options: Activating β2 receptors to cause bronchodilation
scaffolds.explain: Albuterol and similar drugs are β2 AGONISTS — they activate β2 receptors to cause bronchodilation, opening airways during an asthma attack.

wrongExplains.0: Blocking β2 would prevent bronchodilation — the opposite of what's needed in asthma.
wrongExplains.2: Muscarinic activation would cause bronchoconstriction — making asthma worse.
wrongExplains.3: Rescue inhalers specifically target β2 receptors to open airways.

scaffolds.q: Summary: β2 receptor stimulation in bronchi causes ___.
scaffolds.explain: β2 receptor stimulation causes BRONCHODILATION — airways widen. This is the sympathetic response that increases airflow during stress or exercise.

wrongExplains.0: Bronchoconstriction is caused by parasympathetic activation (muscarinic receptors), not β2 stimulation.
wrongExplains.2: β2 stimulation definitely affects airways — it causes significant dilation.
wrongExplains.3: Stimulation of β2 opens airways, it doesn't close them.
